assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend receivable creates a foreign currency exposure because it is denominated in a currency different from a relevant functional currency.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in the fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction, including qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
